title: BABIES FIRST! - 211info
text: TILLAMOOK COUNTY COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTERS PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 2111 8th Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 842-3943 .. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main (503) 842-3943 Allison Bonato [email protected] Alternate (503) 842-3900 ext 3931 Rochelle Phillips [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or visit website FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Tillamook County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at 8th Street and Pacific Avenue Services: Case/Care Management * Families with Infants/Toddlers Home Based Parenting Education Nutrition Education * Families with Infants/Toddlers Prenatal/Postnatal Home Visitation Programs * At Risk Families Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - FOOD HANDLER CARD ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - WATER QUALITY ASSURANCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CACOON PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM PUBLIC HEALTH - PUBLIC HEALTH FACILITY HEALTH INSPECTIONS SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - HARM REDUCTION AND SYRINGE SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - NALOXBOX If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
